importerCapabilities.runtimePolicy: Game runtime code consumes cooked artifacts and must not parse external source formats directly.

presetDocument.policy: Project defaults and per-AssetKeyV2 texture, mesh, and audio preset overrides remain reviewed first-party intent rows. Selected MK_tools glTF mesh, morph mesh CPU, and node transform animation import paths consume mesh unit_scale/up_axis to bake supported data into the project right-handed +Y-up meter convention. Texture/audio presets and unsupported transform paths remain value-only or fail closed; unsupported combinations block import review instead of being ignored.

regressionCorpus.legalPolicy: Corpus rows are commercial evidence candidates only when source paths are safe relative paths, expected hashes are present, third-party rows include expected_sha256, GameEngine.AssetImportProvenance.v1 validation passes, notice_complete=true, external_engine_material=false, and license_policy is accepted_for_source_tree or accepted_for_host_corpus_only. Rejected, unknown, non-commercial, no-derivatives, missing-notice, external-engine, duplicate, and row-budget-overrun rows fail closed.
regressionCorpus.presetPolicy: Mesh corpus rows carry the reviewed mesh unit_scale/up_axis/triangulate/normal/tangent/material-extraction preset intent. The MK_tools runner resolves AssetImportPresets.v1 project defaults/overrides through review_asset_import_preset_for_asset before execution and records a preset SHA-256 row for diagnosis.
regressionCorpus.runnerPolicy: run_asset_import_regression_corpus validates the corpus before importer execution, resolves source paths under a caller-supplied corpus root, records source SHA-256 and preset SHA-256, executes existing first-party import plans and optional ExternalAssetImportAdapters through an overlay filesystem for deterministic double-run evidence, writes only to caller-supplied output_root when requested, and returns value-only GameEngine.AssetImportRegressionReport.v1 rows without leaking parser, KTX, OpenEXR, miniaudio, GPU, native handle, or third-party types. MK_asset_import_regression_runner is a reviewed CLI for repository-relative corpus/output/presets/report paths only; it rejects network URLs, absolute paths, parent traversal, reparse points, unknown arguments, and zero row budgets before execution. tools/run-asset-import-regression-corpus.ps1 runs the existing corpus check first, invokes the CLI, reruns it in a fresh process for report equality, retains report/stdout/stderr/hash evidence, and passes -RequireReady only to the final validator. tools/plan-asset-import-regression-corpus-handoff.ps1 is read-only operator handoff planning: it inspects the host corpus root, manifest, expected hashes, notices, canonical source layout, official-source ledger, corpus-selection summary, top-level report, retained success/failure reports, and local runner presence, then emits asset_import_regression_handoff_* counters plus next commands for manifest generation, dependency bootstrap/build, corpus run, operator loop, RequireReady validation, or Task 17 promotion; it keeps external-engine, Unity/Unreal/Godot compatibility, and legal-approval claims at zero and fails closed under -RequireReady. GameEngine.AssetImportRegressionTriage.v1 maps failed/legal-blocked/nondeterministic report rows to first-party recommended actions, blocked or dry_run_allowed reimport decisions, preset diff and axis/unit preview flags, sanitized repro command ids, and self-contained failed_count/legal_blocked_count/nondeterministic_count header counters without serializing third-party source snippets. tools/check-asset-import-regression-operator-loop.ps1 reads retained report text or synthetic reports, emits operator-loop counters, writes triage.geoperator with the same failed/legal/nondeterministic aggregate counters, and invokes focused asset/editor-core tests under -SyntheticSmoke. Its -CorpusRoot / -RequireReady mode consumes retained/**/report.gereport only, rejects inconsistent report counters and reparse/unsafe retained paths, requires at least one retained success report and one retained failure report before ready corpus-gate output, emits asset_import_regression_operator_loop_corpus_* counters, and fails closed with require_ready.retained_success_report_missing or require_ready.retained_failure_report_missing when evidence is incomplete. The native MK_editor shell accepts only --asset-import-regression-report project-relative report paths, sanitizes visible retained report rows before projecting them into Source Pulse import workflow rows, emits editor_asset_import_regression_* smoke counters, and redacts unsafe source paths. Batch reimport planning, preset diff review, axis/unit preview, operator triage rows, handoff rows, and visible shell report rows are value-only planner surfaces; the editor workflow projects them into Source Pulse retained UI without executing importers, mutating packages, exposing native handles, or claiming external-engine compatibility.
unsupportedClaims: commercial readiness promotion without real corpus run evidence

reviewOnlyRows: KTX2/Basis evidence rows may be reviewed when supplied, but no first-party KTX importer adapter is implemented by this row and KTX rows require exact vcpkg.ktx dependency/legal evidence before readiness.

Selected glTF scene import review rows may be reviewed when supplied with exact vcpkg.asset-importers dependency/legal evidence, source-root policy, parser validation, geometry/material/animation payload evidence, external resource policy, source provenance, and package output rows, but broad scene import readiness, runtime source parsing, external network fetches, parser type leakage, native handle exposure, and package mutation remain unsupported.

Selected source image/audio codec review rows may be reviewed when supplied with exact vcpkg.libspng and vcpkg.miniaudio dependency/legal evidence, source-root policy, PNG RGBA8 pixel-format diagnostics, audio sample-format diagnostics, source provenance, deterministic hashes, and package output rows, but SVG/vector decoding, broad image/audio codec readiness, background decode streaming, HRTF/middleware, runtime source parsing, native handle exposure, and package mutation remain unsupported.

Selected shader generation/cache rows may be reviewed when supplied with exact DXC D3D12 DXIL and DXC Vulkan SPIR-V offline compiler commands, SPIR-V Tools spirv-val --target-env vulkan1.3 validation commands, inputs, outputs, target profiles/environments, cache keys, provenance, diagnostics, and package evidence. Live shader generation, runtime compiler execution, native PSO/Vulkan/Metal cache handles, renderer/RHI residency, Metal library generation, package mutation, and broad shader readiness remain unsupported or host-gated.
unsupportedClaims: package mutation from importer review rows
